author: w3Lb
content: Delivered in commit 8d72e5e: drop(reader) after the read loop, before backup/persist — the input handle held across tmp.persist() is what Windows refuses (os error 5); Unix rename is indifferent, hence green local/Linux/macOS runs. CHANGELOG entry folded into the still-untagged [0.3.1]. Verified: 25 suites green, clippy/fmt clean, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc cargo check passes. Behavioral confirmation requires the Windows runner — watch the next push. PATTERN NOTE for the knowledge base: this is the second Windows-only bug from handle/lock semantics in two days (mandatory LockFileEx regions in #114, open-handle rename denial here); any future code path that renames over a file must audit every handle it holds first.
